name: vector-coder
description: Right-click any function → describe changes in plain English → VECTOR modifies, verifies (5 layers), and shows a diff. 100% local, offline, hallucination-free. Auto-detects Ollama, MLX, and GGUF models on your system.

vector.pythonPath.description: Path to Python 3.10+ interpreter. Auto-detected on first run.

vector.agentPath.description: Absolute path to tsdc-agent/main.py. Leave empty for auto-detection.

enumDescriptions: Auto-detect: tries MLX on Apple Silicon, then Ollama, then llama.cpp
MLX: Apple Silicon native (fastest on Mac M1/M2/M3/M4)
vector.backend.description: Which inference backend to use. 'auto' scans your system and picks the best one.

vector.ollamaUrl.description: Ollama server URL. Only needed if running Ollama on a non-default port.

vector.ollamaModel.description: Ollama model name. Auto-detected if you have code models installed.

vector.maxAttempts.description: Maximum verification attempts before giving up. Each attempt re-generates and re-verifies through all 5 layers.

vector.showWelcome.description: Show the guided setup walkthrough on first install.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with VECTOR
walkthroughs.description: Set up local, offline, verified code modification in 5 minutes.

steps.title: Welcome to VECTOR
steps.description:
VECTOR uses a local LLM on your machine to modify code — then verifies every change through 5 deterministic layers before applying it. Zero cloud. Zero API keys. Zero hallucinations reach your code.

**Supported:** Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, Go, Rust, C/C++, TSX

[Learn More](https://github.com/aaditya8979/VECTOR)

steps.title: Scan Your Environment
steps.description:
VECTOR auto-detects local LLMs already on your system — Ollama models, MLX models, and GGUF files. Let's see what you have.

[Scan My System](command:vector.detectEnvironment)

steps.title: Get a Code Model
steps.description:
VECTOR works best with **Qwen 2.5 Coder 7B** but supports any code model.

**Recommended (all platforms):**
```
ollama pull qwen2.5-coder:7b
```

**Mac with Apple Silicon (fastest):**
```
pip install mlx-lm
```

Skip this step if a model was already detected.

[Open Terminal](command:workbench.action.terminal.new)

steps.title: Install Dependencies
steps.description:
VECTOR needs a few Python packages for code analysis.

```
pip install tree-sitter tree-sitter-python networkx watchdog rich click mypy pytest
```

Or install everything at once from the requirements file.

[Open Terminal](command:workbench.action.terminal.new)

steps.title: Initialize Your Project
steps.description:
Build the Code Property Graph (CPG) for your workspace. This maps every function and its relationships.

Open the Command Palette and run **VECTOR: Initialize Project**.

[Initialize Project](command:vector.init)

steps.title: Make Your First Modification
steps.description:
You're all set! Try it:

1. Open any supported source file
2. Press **Cmd+Shift+M** (Mac) or **Ctrl+Shift+M** (Windows/Linux)
3. Select a function and describe what to change
4. Review the diff → Accept or Revert

[Run Health Check](command:vector.healthCheck)
